mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month changes in FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward returns turns positive over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income growth and corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and industrial production indicates a coordinated demand and supply contraction that amplifies recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns beyond the independent effect of either series.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and INDPRO yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal underperforms the univariate RSAFS signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls during a tight labor market signal accelerating wage costs and imminent monetary tightening that compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
regime_hint: tight labor market with UNRATE below 5%
failure_condition: PAYEMS changes fail to show a negative Rank IC with 5-day forward returns when UNRATE is below 5%, or the effect is weaker than in high-unemployment regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals improving manufacturing momentum and capacity utilization, supporting corporate revenue expansion and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes exhibit a negative Rank IC with 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1250 thousand units signals a contraction in residential construction and forward-looking demand, reducing earnings for cyclical sectors and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when HOUST is below 1250, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is negative over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated consumer price inflation and a rising federal funds rate creates a stagflationary impulse that compresses real corporate earnings and increases equity risk premiums, producing negative returns stronger than either variable in isolation.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to produce a statistically significant negative coefficient (p > 0.05) predicting 60-day returns, or the interaction IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Nominal retail sales sustained above 450 billion dollars signal durable consumer demand that supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances when RSAFS exceeds 450000, or the signal's Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year window.
